danilo_source_note: 15 tabs total. 5 tabs had partial IonWave-specific content (Formulation Master with ocean-sourced thesis + LMNT comparison, Mineral Profile Spec with Na/K/Mg amounts, Bioavailability Research, Dosing Protocols, Ingredient Deep-Dives). 9 tabs were void/minimal placeholders. Vision: 'Most scientifically rigorous electrolyte formulation — one that could withstand peer review.' Key differentiator: 78 ocean minerals vs synthetic 3-4. Critical intelligence gap: Exact trace mineral amounts supplier-dependent (marked throughout as [VOID — requires CoA]). Heavy green-field build using Phase 4 industry research (optimal ratios, bioavailability by form, competitor analysis LMNT/Liquid IV/Drip Drop/Ultima/Nuun, FDA upper limits, ocean-sourced vs synthetic research, René Quinton marine plasma).

phase_4_research_summary.optimal_ratios: Na:K:Mg 17:3:1 (1000:200:60mg) validated by Volek & Phinney keto research + LMNT formulation. Sweat loss averages 220:63:16:8 (Na:K:Ca:Mg). Target population: keto/fasting/performance (2-3g supplemental sodium daily).
phase_4_research_summary.bioavailability_hierarchy: Magnesium forms: Glycinate (40-50%) > Citrate (30%) > Chloride (12%) > Oxide (4-5%). Ocean minerals = chloride/sulfate blend (lower bioavailability than citrate/glycinate, but natural 78-mineral spectrum = trade-off accepted). Marine biocenosis theory: phytoplankton pre-chelate minerals → enhanced bioavailability (Grade B-C evidence, no head-to-head RCT).
phase_4_research_summary.competitor_analysis: LMNT (1000:200:60, stevia, $2.19/serving, keto-focused), Liquid IV (500-630 Na, 370-390 K, 0 Mg, 11g sugar, CTT®, $1.50/serving), Drip Drop (330 Na, 185 K, 11 Mg, 7g sugar, ORS formula), Ultima (55 Na, 250 K, 100 Mg, stevia, $0.83/serving), Nuun (300 Na, 150 K, 25 Mg, effervescent tablets, $0.75/serving). IonWave positioning: LMNT clone with ocean-sourced upgrade, premium $2.50/serving justified by 78-mineral story (value proposition = values-based, not purely evidence-based).
phase_4_research_summary.fda_upper_limits: No UL for sodium (FDA guideline <2,300mg daily). Potassium: no UL for healthy individuals (FDA regulatory 99mg supplement limit is not toxicology-based). Magnesium: 350mg UL from supplements (not including food). IonWave = 1,000 Na (43% of guideline), 200 K (4% RDA), 60 Mg (14% DV, 17% of UL) — wide safety margins.
phase_4_research_summary.ocean_vs_synthetic: Ocean minerals: pre-chelated by phytoplankton (marine biocenosis), ionic form (already dissolved), full spectrum (78 elements). Synthetic: mined/chemically synthesized, simple salts (chloride/citrate/oxide), 3-4 minerals only. Evidence: Marine plasma (Quinton isotonic/hypertonic) has historical clinical use (1897-1925), modern studies show 'enhanced bioavailability' for marine-derived Mg/Ca (mechanism unclear, manufacturer claims). Grade C evidence: plausible but unproven superiority.
phase_4_research_summary.rene_quinton_research: Historical (1897-1925): Replaced 66-104% of dogs' blood with isotonic seawater (animals survived). Treated infants with subcutaneous marine plasma injections (1905 outcomes documented). Modern: No FDA-approved uses, lacks contemporary RCTs. Quinton isotonic (9‰) vs hypertonic (33‰) — IonWave = hypertonic equivalent (user dilutes powder).
sources: Magnesium bioavailability studies (citrate 5.13% blood increase, glycinate 4.88%)
Competitor websites (LMNT, Liquid IV, Drip Drop, Ultima, Nuun)

personas: Formulation Scientist (challenges mineral ratios, bioavailability claims, sourcing purity, stability)
Skeptical Nutritionist (questions ocean-sourced superiority, demands RCTs, critiques trace mineral necessity)
Regulatory/Claims Auditor (flags unsubstantiated claims, checks FDA compliance, verifies safety warnings)
dialogue_summary.saturation_signal: Round 6 focused on strategic planning (RCT scenarios, polypharmacy guidance, post-market surveillance) rather than fundamental formulation flaws. Diminishing returns achieved.
key_upgrades: Marine biocenosis reality check (processing may reduce pre-chelation, but natural 78-mineral ratios = true differentiator)
Bioavailability trade-off analysis (IonWave prioritizes full-spectrum over maximum single-nutrient absorption)
Value proposition clarity (premium justified by ocean sourcing, sustainability, values-based purchase — NOT purely evidence-based)
Heavy metal testing every batch (not random 1 in 10 — mandatory for ocean sourcing)
Hangover protocol disclaimer (reducing alcohol = only proven prevention, IonWave addresses dehydration not toxicity)
Kidney disease contraindication specificity (eGFR <60, CKD Stage 3-5)
ACE inhibitor warning strength (DO NOT USE without physician supervision, not just 'consult')
RCT outcome scenarios (superior/equivalent/inferior bioavailability → strategic pivots documented)
Adverse event surveillance protocol (log complaints, monthly review, thresholds trigger investigation/halt)

self_grade.rationale: Strong evidence coverage for core electrolytes (A-grade). Exceptional confidence honesty (A/B/C/D grades + VOIDs appropriately applied). Clear upgrade paths with WHO/WHAT/WHEN/HOW MUCH. Actionable for PM execution (Week 1-2 supplier outreach, taste testing, stability protocols). OpKit generalizes to any mineral-based supplement. Pre-launch uncertainty (exact trace minerals, taste, stability, bioavailability RCT) keeps score at 8.5 (conservative) vs 9.0+. Target met (8.5-9.0 range).
strengths: Confidence honesty (9.5/10) — no grade inflation, transparent VOIDs
Evidence coverage for core electrolytes (A-grade substantiation)
Safety warnings (kidney disease, ACE inhibitors, hyperkalemia) — rigorous
limitations: Competitive differentiation risk (premium vs LMNT justified by values, not proven performance)

bcl1_progress.cluster_status: 75% complete (M5, M6, M7 done; M8 Brand Identity remains)
